name: auth-mesh-setup
description: CLI toolkit for onboarding any service to the ab0t Auth Mesh. Handles service registration, OAuth clients, hosted login, end-user orgs, consumer registration, and provider setup.
entry_point_description: Interactive CLI — run ./setup for menu, ./setup run for all steps, ./setup run 04 for a specific step.

service_onboarding.description: Register your service with Auth Mesh. Steps 01-06 run in order.
service_onboarding.who: Any service operator onboarding a new service

consume_other_services.description: Register as a consumer of upstream mesh services to get API keys for cross-service calls.
consume_other_services.who: Any service that needs to call other services' APIs

provide_to_other_services.description: Open your service so other mesh services can self-register as consumers.
provide_to_other_services.who: Any service that wants to be consumed by other services

setup.intent: Interactive CLI entry point. Shows menu, runs steps, checks status.

01-register-service-permissions.intent: Creates your service org, admin account, permission schema, and API key on the auth service.

02-register-oauth-client.intent: Registers an OAuth 2.1 public client (PKCE) for your frontend app.

03-setup-hosted-login.intent: Configures the hosted login page — branding, registration settings, auth methods.

04-setup-default-team.intent: Creates the end-users org where humans self-register. Sets up the Default Users team with auto-join so new users get permissions immediately via Zanzibar.

05-verify-setup.intent: Health checks — verifies all orgs, teams, login config, and permissions are correctly configured.

06-test-end-user.intent: End-to-end test — registers a new user, verifies they auto-join the team and get permissions.

07-register-consumer.intent: YOU WANT TO CALL OTHER SERVICES' APIs. Iterates provider configs and registers your service as a consumer of each. Produces API keys for cross-service calls.

08-setup-api-consumers.intent: YOU WANT OTHER SERVICES TO CALL YOUR APIs. Creates a consumer registration org with permission tier teams. After this, other services self-register with two API calls.

register-as-client.intent: Co-located registration engine. Reads provider credential files from disk, logs in as provider admin, creates sub-org + service account + API key. Use when consumer and provider are on the same machine.

consumer-register.intent: Self-service registration engine. Consumer registers via org-scoped endpoint, auto-joins permission team, creates API key. Does NOT need provider credentials. Supports convention-based discovery or invitation files.

provider-accept-consumer.intent: Provider-side setup for a specific consumer. Creates sub-org, approved-permissions team, auto-join config, and outputs an invitation file. The consumer uses consumer-register.sh with the invitation.

permissions.json.intent: Defines your service's permission schema — actions, resources, default grants. Used by steps 01, 04, and 08.

oauth-client.json.intent: OAuth 2.1 client registration — redirect URIs, grant types, scopes. Used by step 02.

hosted-login.json.intent: Hosted login page branding and registration settings. Used by step 03.

api-consumers.json.intent: Consumer tier definitions — which permissions each tier grants. Used by step 08. Auto-generated from permissions.json if missing.

clients.d/*.json.intent: Per-provider configs — declares which upstream service to consume and what permissions to request. One file per provider.

credentials.description: Output files created by setup scripts. All gitignored — contain real secrets. Never commit these.

files.{service}.json: Step 01 output — service org, admin account, API key
files.oauth-client.json: Step 02 output — OAuth client ID and registration token
files.hosted-login.json: Step 03 output — hosted login config snapshot
files.end-users-org.json: Step 04 output — end-users org ID, default team ID, login URL
files.{provider}-consumer.json: Step 07 output — consumer API key for calling upstream provider
files.api-consumers.json: Step 08 output — consumer org ID, tier team IDs, registration endpoint

schemas.intent: JSON Schema definitions for all config and credential files. Used by validate-config.sh.

skills.intent: Claude Code skill definitions. Provide context to AI assistants working with this repo.

skills.auth-mesh-setup: How to onboard a service to Auth Mesh (steps 01-07)
skills.mesh-service-accounts: How to register as a consumer of other mesh services

tests.note: UJ tests live in the service repo, not in setup/. See sandbox-platform/scripts/curl_tests/user_journeys/
tests.intent: Integration tests that verify the sandbox-platform API works end-to-end. Run against a live instance. Located in the service directory, not the setup CLI.
